framework.name: NIST Privacy Framework
framework.disclaimer: NIST Privacy Framework is voluntary U.S. government guidance and is in the public domain. Shown here at the Function and Category level for reference; verify against the official NIST publication. Not legal advice or a compliance determination.

controls.title: Identify-P
controls.statement: Identify-P function of the NIST Privacy Framework.

children.title: Inventory and Mapping
children.statement: Inventory the systems, products, services, and data processing activities, and map data flows, so the organization understands what personal data it handles and where it goes.

children.title: Business Environment
children.statement: Understand the organization's role in the data processing ecosystem, its mission, and stakeholders, and align privacy priorities with business objectives.

children.title: Risk Assessment
children.statement: Assess the privacy risks to individuals that arise from data processing, including the likelihood and impact of problematic data actions.

children.title: Data Processing Ecosystem Risk Management
children.statement: Identify and manage privacy risks that come from third parties, service providers, and partners in the data processing ecosystem.

controls.title: Govern-P
controls.statement: Govern-P function of the NIST Privacy Framework.

children.title: Governance Policies, Processes, and Procedures
children.statement: Establish and maintain the organizational privacy values, policies, roles, and legal/regulatory understanding that guide privacy risk management.

children.title: Risk Management Strategy
children.statement: Establish the organization's risk tolerance and a strategy for prioritizing and responding to privacy risk.

children.title: Awareness and Training
children.statement: Ensure staff and third parties understand their privacy roles, responsibilities, and the organization's privacy policies.

children.title: Monitoring and Review
children.statement: Continuously monitor and review the privacy posture, legal environment, and effectiveness of the privacy program, adjusting as needed.

controls.title: Control-P
controls.statement: Control-P function of the NIST Privacy Framework.

children.title: Data Processing Policies, Processes, and Procedures
children.statement: Establish policies and procedures that let the organization manage data processing consistent with its privacy strategy, including data minimization and purpose limitation.

children.title: Data Processing Management
children.statement: Manage data elements through their lifecycle so they can be accessed for review, transmission, alteration, and deletion, and are destroyed according to policy.

children.title: Disassociated Processing
children.statement: Apply techniques such as de-identification, aggregation, and attribute substitution so processing can occur without unnecessarily linking data to individuals.

controls.title: Communicate-P
controls.statement: Communicate-P function of the NIST Privacy Framework.

children.title: Communication Policies, Processes, and Procedures
children.statement: Establish policies and assign roles for communicating clearly, internally and externally, about the organization's data processing and privacy practices.

children.title: Data Processing Awareness
children.statement: Enable individuals and stakeholders to understand how data is processed, maintain records of disclosures, and design systems for data processing visibility.

controls.title: Protect-P
controls.statement: Protect-P function of the NIST Privacy Framework.

children.title: Data Protection Policies, Processes, and Procedures
children.statement: Maintain data protection policies including configuration change control, backups, response and recovery plans, HR practices, and a vulnerability management plan.

children.title: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
children.statement: Limit access to data and devices to authorized users and processes, applying least privilege, separation of duties, and managed remote and physical access.

children.title: Data Security
children.statement: Protect data at rest and in transit, guard against data leaks, maintain availability, and use integrity checking for software, firmware, and hardware.

children.title: Maintenance
children.statement: Perform maintenance and repairs on systems that process personal data in a controlled, logged, and authorized manner.

children.title: Protective Technology
children.statement: Manage protective technology such as removable media restrictions, least functionality, and protected communications and control networks.
